seoTitle: Old Plains vs Oldbury — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Old Plains and Oldbury. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Old Plains vs Oldbury: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Old Plains and Oldbury.
heading: Old Plains vs Oldbury — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Old Plains, Western Australia and Oldbury, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Old Plains has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Oldbury (34/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Old Plains or Oldbury safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Old Plains has a flood risk score of 28/100, while Oldbury scores 40/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Old Plains scores 23/100 for bushfire risk. Oldbury scores 18/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Old Plains or Oldbury?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Old Plains has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.6% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
